content_markdown: A standing wave is produced by the superposition of two travelling waves. Which statement is **not** correct?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: The travelling waves must have the same frequency.

options.label: B
options.text: The travelling waves must have equal but opposite velocities.

options.label: C
options.text: In the standing wave, all the oscillating particles have the same amplitude.

options.label: D
options.text: In the standing wave, particles between adjacent nodes vibrate in phase.

vibe_explanation: A standing wave is formed by the interference of two waves of the same frequency and amplitude traveling in opposite directions. Unlike a travelling wave where every particle has the same amplitude, a standing wave has nodes (zero amplitude) and antinodes (maximum amplitude), meaning the amplitude varies depending on the position along the wave. Therefore, statement C is incorrect.
